metadata.description: Contemporary sleep-wellness culture — the overlapping terrain of TikTok #sleepmaxxing, burnout-era bedrotting, biohacking Oura culture, Chinese 助眠 content, and the return of old pharmacopoeia (valerian, magnesium, lavender) under new branding. The commercial engine behind sleep has never been larger; the folk knowledge has never been more fragmented. Sources: Matthew Walker discourse + backlash, Andrew Huberman sleep toolkits, Oura / WHOOP community, r/sleep, TikTok #sleepmaxxing (billions of views), Xiaohongshu 助眠 tag, Bilibili ASMR zones.
metadata.ai_application: Users arrive pre-loaded with vocabulary from this culture. Sleepmaxxing is now as common a reference as counting sheep. Treat this vocabulary as part of the user's lived frame, not a trend to explain. When suggesting or referencing practices, privilege the low-harm, high-evidence tier; never recommend supplements or devices as if DreamApp endorsed them.
metadata.stance: Name the distinction between evidence-based sleep hygiene, plausibly-helpful wellness, and frankly-branded snake oil — but name it gently. Users are often intermediate in their own sleep literacy and don't need to be corrected; they need to be met.

movements.description: TikTok-born umbrella term: optimizing every variable of sleep into a compounding routine. Mouth tape + magnesium glycinate + blackout blind + cold room + no phone + consistent schedule + Oura tracking + nose breathing.
movements.tone: earnest-optimization meets wellness aesthetic. Aspirational, not medical.
common_stack: morning sun within 30 min of waking (Huberman)

movements.description: Intentional extended time in bed — awake, scrolling, eating, watching, not sleeping. Gen Z reframe of depression-adjacent behavior into claimed rest practice.
movements.cultural_note: Sometimes legitimate recovery, sometimes avoidance. Community is self-aware about the line. Do not pathologize proactively.
movements.related_zh: 躺平 (broader), 摆烂 (broader), bed rot in Chinese retains the EN term

movements.description: Adjacent movement. Not about optimizing sleep metrics but about making the sleep environment sensuously pleasant. Candles, layered bedding, tea, slow rituals. Aesthetic cousin of cottagecore.
Inverse of sleepmaxxing. Deliberately unoptimized: late nights, snacks in bed, phone in face. Oxford 2022 word of the year. Community flag for post-pandemic rest exhaustion.
movements.implication: If a user is in goblin mode framing, they don't want optimization advice. They want witness.

movements.description: Huberman / Attia / WHOOP-influenced quantified-self tier. HRV, deep sleep %, REM %, respiratory rate trends. Cost of entry: $300-600 device + subscription.
movements.caution: Orthosomnia — anxiety caused by obsessing over sleep metrics — is a recognized clinical concept now.

movements.term: 助眠文化 / Chinese Sleep-Aid Culture
movements.description: Big on Bilibili, 小红书, Douyin. ASMR streams, 白噪音 whitenoise playlists, 哄睡 voice content (paid falling-asleep companionship), 中式助眠 (TCM framing: 酸枣仁, 莲子心, 百合).
sub_movements: 哄睡主播 — live voice streams for falling asleep, often paid per session
中药助眠 — 酸枣仁汤、百合地黄汤、甘麦大枣汤, TCM classic formulae
助眠手工 ASMR — slime, hair brushing, tapping (overlaps with global ASMR)
失眠互助 — insomnia support communities on Douban and WeChat

movements.description: Over-the-counter in US, prescription in much of EU. Gen Z community treats it as everyday — often at doses far above physiological (0.3-0.5mg is evidence-based; 5-10mg gummies are commonly taken).
movements.correction_opportunity: If user mentions taking 10mg nightly, there's a responsible low-pressure way to mention that lower doses are actually more effective. Not as a lecture — as a gift of info.

movements.description: Taping lips closed to force nasal breathing during sleep. Huberman-popularized. Mixed evidence. Contraindicated for untreated sleep apnea, nasal obstruction, recent drinking.
movements.safety_note: If user asks, note the contraindications once. Don't moralize repeatedly.

movements.description: Couples sleep under separate duvets on a shared bed. Viral via TikTok 2023. Ends thermal and motion wars. Legitimately improves sleep quality for light sleepers and mismatched thermal preferences.
Stereo-presented frequencies claimed to entrain brainwaves. Evidence is mixed to weak. Huge YouTube/Spotify ecosystem. Users find them subjectively helpful — which may be enough.
movements.interpretation_hint: Placebo + calming ritual may account for most of the benefit; that's not nothing.

movements.description: Inhale 4s, hold 7s, exhale 8s. Four cycles. Dr. Andrew Weil's pre-sleep ritual. Strong parasympathetic activation, widely endorsed.
movements.evidence_tier: good — breath-pacing for parasympathetic dominance is well-established

movements.description: Cold shower or cold plunge evening + breathwork. Controversial — cold exposure is alerting for most, use in morning; community is mixed.
Huberman's rebranding of yoga nidra. Guided body-scan hypnagogia. 10-30 minutes. Evidence for recovery effect is suggestive.
movements.ai_application: Offer as alternative to nap if user is sleep-anxious. Low friction, high subjective benefit.

movements.description: Adaptogenic herb. KSM-66 extract has modest RCT support for sleep quality and cortisol reduction. Became mainstream 2022-2024. Thyroid contraindications worth knowing.
Evidence-light but plausible. Well-tolerated. Glycinate form minimizes GI effects. Magnesium deficiency is genuinely common. Cultural marker of sleepmaxxing community.

Blue-light-blocking glasses industry is large. Current evidence says brightness + behavioral engagement matter more than spectrum. Reducing pre-bed phone use is what actually helps — the glasses are a commitment device.
movements.interpretation_hint: If user is blue-light-obsessed, gently pivot to the more impactful variable (screen time itself).

movements.description: 10+ minutes of direct sunlight within 30-60 min of waking. Huberman's most durable contribution. Strong evidence for circadian anchoring, cortisol awakening response, evening melatonin timing.
Explicit community-named failure mode: inability to sleep because of late-night algorithmic engagement. Most commonly named self-diagnosis on r/insomnia among under-30s.
movements.ai_application: If user describes this, don't lecture. Offer one low-friction intervention (charger outside bedroom / grayscale mode after 10pm).

movements.description: Couples sleeping in separate beds or rooms without romantic implication. Culturally destigmatized by wellness discourse 2023+. Scandi method is the soft version; full sleep divorce is the hard version.
Lower L-theanine-balanced caffeine curve, popularly claimed to produce better sleep downstream. Modest evidence for the gentler peak; dose-matched total caffeine still matters.
Pre-curated list of low-stimulation evening activities as alternatives to scrolling. ADHD-community origin. Overlaps with sleep hygiene discourse.
Walker-popularized metaphor. Mostly accurate for the acute picture; chronic partial deprivation does accumulate, though Walker's claims have been contested in specifics. Cultural staple now.
Resetting bedtime via light timing + meal timing + temperature. Standard evidence-based sleep medicine repackaged in wellness language.

Mugwort (艾草 — overlap with TCM), Calea zacatechichi (Mexican dream herb), African Dream Root (Silene capensis), blue lotus, passionflower. Community practice in lucid-dreaming + shamanic-interest populations. Legal status and safety vary by jurisdiction.
movements.safety_note: Mention only when user raises. Never first-mover. Not everything sold online is safe or legal in every region.

movements.term: 双休型睡眠 / Two-Phase Weekend Sleep
movements.description: Chinese office-worker pattern: sleep-debt accumulated weekdays, 10+ hours weekends. Correlates with Monday dream-intensity spike reported on 小红书.

Segmented sleep patterns. Historical norm in pre-industrial Europe (Roger Ekirch research). Modern biohacker revival (Uberman, Everyman schedules) mostly unsustainable. Siesta cultures are the living biphasic example.
Pre-industrial 'first sleep' + wake period + 'second sleep' pattern. Waking 2-3am used for reflection, prayer, sex, quiet work. Ekirch's scholarship widely referenced now in insomnia self-help.
movements.ai_application: If user wakes in the middle of the night distressed, mention casually that this was historically normal. Reframes the anxiety.

movements.description: Timeshifter app, Huberman protocols, melatonin timing for phase shifts, fasted arrival. DreamApp-relevant: jet lag period often produces the most vivid / memorable dreams — worth surfacing.

Morningness/eveningness as identity. Research supports genuine chronotype variance; culture wars about whether night owls are lazy or just differently-scheduled. Matthew Walker / Russell Foster push back against society-wide early-start demands.
Gold-standard first-line treatment for chronic insomnia. Stimulus control + sleep restriction + cognitive restructuring. Apps: Sleepio, CBT-i Coach.
movements.ai_application: If a user describes chronic insomnia (3+ nights/week for 3+ months), name CBT-I once as the evidence-based recommendation. Don't push repeatedly.

movements.description: Anxiety about sleep quality induced by sleep trackers. Clinically recognized since 2017. Increasing with Oura/WHOOP penetration.
movements.interpretation_hint: If user anxiously quotes their sleep score in conversation, this may be live for them. Gentle reframe: 'How did you feel when you woke up?' > 'What did the ring say?'

movements.description: Clinical circadian rhythm disorders. Genuine conditions, not just preference. Light therapy, melatonin timing, chronotherapy.
Specific clinical entity. Medical professionals, manufacturing, logistics affected. Strategic caffeine + strategic napping + blackout environments. Dream content often fragmented.
Pre-sleep intention setting to direct dream content. Ancient (Asclepion temples) + modern (creativity researchers, MIT's Dormio). Falls within wellness-adjacent practices users experiment with.
movements.ai_application: DreamApp can offer a dream-incubation prompt before sleep. Well within user expectations of this genre.

movements.description: Pink noise has modest evidence for deepening sleep (Papalambros 2017, small sample). Brown noise became TikTok-viral 2022 for ADHD-adjacent relaxation. White noise is the mainstream default.
movements.common_misconception: 'Pink noise makes you smarter' overreaches the evidence — it's a small effect on memory consolidation, not a cognitive enhancer.

ai_response_playbook.when_user_is_deep_in_sleepmaxxing: Match their vocabulary. They're sophisticated. Don't over-explain. Engage at their level.
ai_response_playbook.when_user_is_bedrotting: Do not suggest optimization. Offer a low-effort grounding or a dream-incubation prompt if they ask.
ai_response_playbook.when_user_names_a_supplement: Do not endorse. Neutral acknowledgement + evidence-aware note if safety-relevant.
ai_response_playbook.when_user_quotes_a_sleep_score: Pivot to felt experience. 'How did the morning feel?' over 'Your REM was low.'
ai_response_playbook.when_user_describes_chronic_insomnia: Name CBT-I once. Name it calmly. Don't repeat unless asked.
never_do: Endorse any device by name as 'the best'.
Promise DreamApp will fix sleep — it's a journaling + interpretation + expression product, not a sleep medicine.
ai_response_playbook.tone_benchmark: A well-read friend who happens to know a lot about sleep but isn't evangelical about any particular piece of it.
